name: Little Rock AFB
tagline: The 'Home of C-130 Combat Airlift,' the world's largest C-130 base, near Little Rock, Arkansas.
_note: Air Force airlift base (Jacksonville, AR, Little Rock metro; Central time). CONUS full template (BAH MHA AR010 Little Rock; RentCast/SchoolDigger work). 19th Airlift Wing + 314th Airlift Wing (C-130 training); the world's largest C-130 base and the hub for C-130 aircrew training. Medical: 19th Medical Group clinic. Facts verified from official sources (littlerock.af.mil, 19 MDG/TRICARE, DeCA/AAFES, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Arkansas moved to TRICARE West in 2024. Affordable. Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx), off-base ZIP 72076 (Jacksonville).

data.Aircraft: C-130 Hercules (combat airlift and aircrew training)
data.Distinction: The world's largest C-130 base and the C-130 training hub

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Little Rock Air Force Base is the 'Home of C-130 Combat Airlift,' the world's largest C-130 base. The 19th Airlift Wing flies operational missions while the 314th Airlift Wing trains C-130 aircrews from across the U.S. and partner nations.
It's in Jacksonville, in the Little Rock metro of central Arkansas, an affordable area with a settled, family-friendly feel.

data.subtitle: The Little Rock area is affordable and family-friendly. The main choice is which nearby community fits your commute and schools, with on-base housing also available.
intro: Central Arkansas is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well. On-base housing is available; many also live off base around Jacksonville, Cabot, and the metro.
Where you live mostly comes down to commute and schools, with Cabot a favorite of families and Little Rock proper offering big-city amenities.

recommendations.advice: Jacksonville borders the base for the shortest commute.
Cabot is popular with families for its schools, a short drive northeast.
On-base housing is available and convenient; weigh it against the affordable local market.
Little Rock and North Little Rock offer dining, shopping, and an airport a short drive south.

sections.title: Gates & Access
data: Visitors without a DoD ID need a visitor pass and a REAL ID-compliant ID from the Visitor Control Center.
Gate hours and visitor requirements change, so confirm the current details on the official Little Rock AFB site before you go.

sections.title: On-Base Housing
data: Little Rock AFB has privatized on-base family housing across several neighborhoods.
Eligibility and availability vary by rank and family size, so start with the housing office for the current manager, options, and waitlists.

source.label: DoD 2026 BAH Rates, MHA AR010 (Little Rock)

sections.title: Schools
data: There is no DoDEA district here; children attend the local civilian public schools. Jacksonville/North Pulaski, Cabot (well regarded), and Pulaski County districts serve military families depending on where you live.
Your assigned schools depend on your address, so confirm the district and campus for any neighborhood you are considering.

data.Care: Outpatient primary and some specialty care on base; inpatient, emergency, and complex care via Little Rock-area network hospitals

intro: Whether to rent or buy is the biggest money decision of most PCS moves, and the affordable Little Rock market makes buying a real option for many families.
rent: Your tour is short (about two years or less) or you're here for a C-130 training pipeline.
You want time to learn the metro before committing.
You'd rather not gamble on resale timing when you PCS out.
Your BAH comfortably covers rent in the areas you'd consider.
buy: You expect to be here three or more years.
You want to use your VA loan and build equity instead of paying rent.
The affordable central Arkansas market fits your budget for a home you like.
You've found a community and commute you're confident about.
data.bottom_line: There is no universal right answer, but the affordable Little Rock market makes buying worth a look for longer tours. Run your BAH against the rent and home prices above, weigh your tour length, and talk to a lender and an agent who work with military families before you commit.

intro: The first month at a new duty station is mostly logistics. Knocking these out early makes everything else easier.

steps.title: Line up temporary lodging
steps.text: Book on-base lodging or a short-term rental before you arrive. You can claim Temporary Lodging Expense (TLE) for a limited number of days while you house-hunt.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, and make sure DEERS and ID cards are current for every family member so TRICARE and base access work.

steps.title: Start with the Housing Office
steps.text: Check on-base availability and get the office's read on Jacksonville, Cabot, and the metro before you sign anything.

steps.title: Register your vehicle and sort access
steps.text: Handle base access and Arkansas vehicle registration, and learn the visitor-pass process for guests. Bring your registration and insurance.

steps.title: Enroll the kids in school
steps.text: Contact the gaining district (Jacksonville/North Pulaski, Cabot, or Pulaski County) early and bring records and immunizations.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Update your TRICARE enrollment so you can be seen at the 19th Medical Group clinic or network providers.

data.q: What is Little Rock AFB known for?
data.a: It's the 'Home of C-130 Combat Airlift,' the world's largest C-130 base, with the 19th Airlift Wing (operational) and the 314th Airlift Wing (C-130 aircrew training).
On-base housing is available; many live off base. Jacksonville is closest; Cabot is popular for schools; Little Rock offers big-city amenities.
Yes. Central Arkansas is inexpensive, so BAH stretches well.
Little Rock AFB is MHA AR010 (Little Rock). Use the calculator above for your grade.
